_meta.description: KB validation test cases derived from real forum questions. Each case has a question, expected answer components from expert responses, and coverage scoring.
_meta.entry_point: Scheduled agents: read this file, run questions against KB, record results in test-results.json

test_cases.title: Rough idle after spark plug change on 2.7 V6
test_cases.question: I have an 06 Tiburon GT with the 2.7 V6. Recently replaced spark plugs and coil packs at 150K miles. Car runs stronger on highway but idle is rough — slight vibration through steering wheel and seats when sitting at a light. No CEL, drives fine once moving. Coil connections seem seated correctly. Is this common with these engines? Should I check vacuum lines, throttle body, or intake?

expected_answer_components.claim: Only use NGK or Denso spark plugs. The 2.7L V6 Delta engine is sensitive to plug brand — Bosch, Autolite, and Champion have incorrect internal resistance for this ignition system and cause misfires or rough idle.

Check the throttle body mounting bracket. There is a bracket between the intake manifold and throttle body that can be incorrectly positioned during reassembly. The bracket goes against the throttle body only (top), not between intake and TB (bottom).
expected_answer_components.gap_note: No KB content on throttle body bracket position or torque. This is common tribal knowledge from Charlie-III.

expected_answer_components.claim: Two similar connectors near the front of the throttle body can be physically swapped but should not be — one pair is black, one is dark grey. Swapping them causes rough running.
expected_answer_components.gap_note: No KB content on TB-area connector swap risk. Shop manual does not warn about this. Charlie-III identifies this as a common mistake.

expected_answer_components.claim: Check that the small ground wire near the rear bank was reattached. It can be disturbed during spark plug access and causes electrical gremlins including rough idle.
expected_answer_components.gap_note: Ground distribution documented in common/electrical-manual/schematics/ but not linked to spark plug service procedure context.

expected_answer_components.claim: Most common problem after a plug job is a vacuum leak. Spray brake cleaner around all intake connection points while the engine is idling — if RPM dips or stumbles, you found the leak location.
expected_answer_components.gap_note: Vacuum leak diagnosis procedure exists in fuel system chapter but may not be cross-linked to ignition/plug service context.

expected_answer_components.claim: Best coil pack brands are Hyundai/Mando OEM or NTK. Many aftermarket coils are substandard and cause rough running even when new.
expected_answer_components.gap_note: Partial coverage — OEM part numbers exist in sensor-information.md but coil brand quality ranking may not be explicit.

key_points: internal resistance mismatch with other brands
vacuum leak — spray brake cleaner method

test_cases.title: Wheel fitment: 18x8.5 ET35 with 225/40-18
test_cases.question: Will 18x8.5 wheels with 35mm offset and 225/40-18 tires rub on a GK Tiburon at stock ride height?

expected_answer_components.claim: Hub-to-rim-lip distance for 18x8.5 ET35 is 2.85 inches, which exceeds the 2.2-inch safe limit for stock fenders.
This combo will rub at stock height. Even with rolled fenders (2.7-inch limit), it's borderline since 2.85 exceeds 2.7.
Stock reference: 17x7 ET46, 215/45-17, hub-to-lip 1.7 inches, hub-to-fender 2.7 inches.
Real-world confirmation: kagame ran 18x8.5 +35 225/40-18 on Tein coilovers and got slight rear rub on bumps. Required inner fender plastic trim and screw removal.
